title: Update .rules and zed-agentops-init.sh heredoc to require workspace initialization before restore
summary: Update the repository .rules file and the scaffolded .rules heredoc in zed-agentops-init.sh so startup requires a one-time workspace_initialize(cwd) call before tx_state, tx_event_log, or handoff restore, while keeping the generated scaffold aligned with the runtime contract for unresolved '/' startup cases.
acceptance_criteria: The root .rules file requires workspace_initialize(cwd) as the first mandatory startup step before any canonical state restore.
The startup order in .rules is updated to workspace_initialize(cwd) -> tx_state -> tx_event_log -> handoff.
The zed-agentops-init.sh embedded .rules heredoc matches the repository .rules contract for workspace initialization and root-dependent tool behavior.
The wording makes clear that workspace initialization happens once, same-root reinitialization is acceptable as a no-op, and root-dependent tools must not run before initialization completes.
The updated scaffold text remains aligned with the runtime safety goal of never resolving artifacts under /.agent.

steps.description: Update the repository .rules Start section so workspace_initialize(cwd) is mandatory before reading tx_state, tx_event_log, or handoff.
Clarify in .rules that root-dependent tools must not run before initialization and that initialization is a one-time per-session bind.
Update the .rules heredoc inside zed-agentops-init.sh to mirror the repository .rules startup contract exactly.
Check the repository copy and scaffolded heredoc for wording drift so future initialized projects receive the same startup instructions.

notes: Keep the repository .rules and scaffolded .rules text synchronized in the same change.
The contract should explicitly support the Zed multi-project case where the server can start unresolved because process CWD is '/'.
Do not weaken the safety guarantee that artifacts must never resolve under /.agent.
